reason: The current iteration cannot run end-to-end due to critical schema and loader mismatches (personas CSV, YAML structures, CRT scoring). These are structural rather than minor bugs, and fixing them in one more iteration should make the simulator executable and evaluable. We are at iteration 2 of 3, so there is room for a focused final iteration that prioritizes schema alignment and minimal but correct functionality over new features.

specific_areas: Implement a robust preprocessing step for synthetic_personas.csv to map raw columns (Age, sex, BFI_*, NFC_Total, CRT2_Total_adj/CRT_Combined) into the internal schema (persona_id, age, gender, bfi_*, nfc_score, crt2_level) used throughout the code.
Fix BFI-10 and NFC-18 YAML parsing by iterating over data['items'], and align item-to-trait mappings and reverse-keying with the actual YAML definitions and scale documentation.

Relax and correct CRT2/bCRT loaders: parse data['items'], accept code-side correct_answer dictionaries, allow missing answers without hard failure, and rely on the simulated correctness flag rather than string equality.
Update Evaluator to operate solely on the preprocessed internal persona schema and verify that trait_fidelity metrics can be computed without KeyErrors.
Ensure admin_mode branches (per_item, per_test, all_tests) are structurally differentiated at the level of batching and metadata, even if response generation remains deterministic.
Run a minimal end-to-end test (small subset of personas) as part of verification to confirm that loading, simulation, and evaluation complete without runtime errors.

task_understanding.adjustments: Emphasize strict adherence to the documented schemas for synthetic_personas.csv and the BFI/NFC/CRT YAMLs. Avoid inventing new internal schemas without an explicit, well-documented mapping layer from the provided data.

data_analysis.adjustments: Prioritize early inspection of actual CSV/YAML fields and structures before designing loaders. Explicitly list required raw columns and YAML keys, and design preprocessing around them instead of assuming preprocessed columns.

model_planning.adjustments: Defer advanced architectural refinements and new features until basic schema alignment and load/run capability are confirmed. Plan the next iteration as a stabilization pass focused on data compatibility and minimal working pipeline.

code_generation.adjustments: Generate code that is instrument-specific where needed (e.g., separate paths for BFI, NFC, CRT) rather than over-generic loaders that guess structures. Include clear comments describing column and field mappings and avoid over-strict validation that depends on data not yet present.

code_verification.adjustments: Add simple, targeted checks: (1) load personas and assert presence of internal columns; (2) load each YAML and assert correct number of items and traits; (3) run a tiny simulation and evaluation on a handful of personas. Treat any runtime error in these steps as blocking for completion of the iteration.

simulation_execution.adjustments: Focus on ensuring that all three test types (BFI, NFC, CRT) can be administered without errors using the existing deterministic logic. Do not add stochastic complexity or new behaviours until all loaders and mappings are stable.

result_evaluation.adjustments: Concentrate on verifying that trait_fidelity metrics use the correct internal columns, and temporarily accept approximate or placeholder accuracy as long as the pipeline runs and outputs interpretable correlations without NaNs or KeyErrors.

feedback_generation.adjustments: No major change needed; continue to prioritize pinpointing schema mismatches and structural blockers, but keep feedback tightly scoped to runnability and correctness for the next (final) iteration.
